name: T-Bar Row
resistance_profile: A close neutral grip on a landmine/T-bar lets you pull heavy through a long arc with the elbows tucked, emphasizing lat and mid-back thickness through the mid-range.
cues: Hinge hard, flat back, chest proud
Pull the handle to the belly, elbows tight
Control the stretch — don't drop the weight
execution_steps: Load one end of a barbell in a landmine (or use a dedicated T-bar row station) and straddle the bar.
Attach a close-grip V-handle under the bar and hinge to a flat-back bent-over position with the chest up.
Lift the weight to arm's length off the floor as your stretched starting position.
Row the handle to your lower chest or upper abdomen, driving the elbows back and squeezing the mid-back.
Lower under control to a full stretch, keeping the hips hinged, and repeat.
good_when: You want to load back thickness heavy with a supported neutral grip.
You want a long-arc row that keeps the elbows tight to the torso.
bad_when: You have a lower back that dislikes loaded bent-over holds.
You want a fully chest-supported option with zero spinal load.
common_errors: Standing up with the hips to jerk the weight
Rounding the lower back at the bottom
Using too little range and half-repping
progressions: Add plates or reps, or pause at the top contraction.
Use a wider grip attachment to shift emphasis onto the upper back.
regressions: Reduce load or elevate the plates to shorten the range.
Switch to a chest-supported T-bar or machine row to remove lower-back load.
